learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate a detailed knowledge of a range of key texts and theories that illustrate important debates and issues in the history of Western theatre
Be able to evaluate complex ideas and themes, and deploy such knowledge in discussions and debates in order to persuade and inform others
Consider how the 'here and now' of contemporary Australian theatre is informed by the complex traditions of the Western theatrical canon
Conceptualise and conduct an independent research project that requires skills in library research, performance analysis, critical thinking, scholarly writing, and time management

assessments.due_date:
7/10/2026 - 14/10/2026

Presentations will take place during timetabled seminars in Week 10 and Week 11.
assessments.task_description:
This assignment is designed to prepare you to present your research projects as papers to peers and staff. It is the sort of activity you can expect to engage in not only in your prospective Honours or postgraduate careers, but also in any kind of professional career that involves public presentation of private research and critical thinking. It can also be considered an early form of lecture delivery training.

You are to use the essays you plan to submit in October as the project you are summarising and presenting to the group for feedback in draft form. This will involve preparing visual aids that summarise the essay/project topic and its argument. Your presentation materials, including visual aids and handouts, can be prepared in the presentation software of your choice in advance of the day of your presentation, and should be submitted via Turnitin by 4pm on the day after your presentation. This task also includes a short post-presentation Q and A.

A detailed assessment brief will be available on Blackboard during the semester.

Recording of Oral and Practical Assessment

  • All presentations may be recorded for marking purposes via recording facilities available where the assessment takes place (eg. ECHO360, Zoom, camera device). Alternatively, they will be double marked.
  • Recordings will be retained by the School of Communication and Arts for at least 12 months from the release of the final grade for the course.
  • Recordings will be stored in a secure manner and will only be accessed by authorised school staff for the purposes of:
  • Moderation of marking;
  • Provision of feedback to the student(s) recorded; and/or
  • Re-marking following a successful re-mark application.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Presented during your Week 10 and 11 seminar slot, presentation materials submitted to Turnitin by 4PM on the day of your presentation.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission is not possible for this piece of assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
For each of the 7 weeks of substantive module material in this course (Weeks 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 12), you will be given 15 minutes to handwrite 100 words of critical writing responding to a specific concept raised in that week's reading material. These may ask for a response to the set play(s) or to the set critical reading(s). Some will require responses to visual and/or audio-visual material.

Students will be marked out of 3 points for 7 weeks, with the strongest 5 counting towards the assessment (3 x 5= 15 marks). Two participation marks for each substantive module teaching week, and .5 marks per presentation week will be awarded for your in-class engagement and demonstration of your comprehension of the week's reading materials.

Because only 5 of the 7 written responses count, the assessment already builds in the flexibility to miss up to 2 sessions — for illness or any other reason — with no penalty and no documentation required. Alternative assessment isn't available except in specific circumstances such as illness or exceptional circumstances with appropriate documentation. To be considered for a make-up session or any other alternative arrangement, you must contact the course coordinator with your documentation as soon as possible after the circumstances arise. If you have a Disability Action Plan relevant to this task, please contact the course coordinator as early as possible. Reasonable accommodations can be made for matters documented in a Disability Action Plan.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission is not possible for this piece of assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
You are to devise your own research question arising from the "Immersion and Interactivity", "The Medea Myth", and "Causing Controversy" modules, or the other historiographical material that we have covered across this course. Your written essay should respond to the feedback you receive on your in-class presentation. 

The referencing style used for Drama assignments is MLA, 9th edition.

A detailed assignment brief will be provided on Blackboard at the beginning of Semester.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 1: Introduction to Theatre Historiography

This seminar will introduce the course, discuss course scheduling, assessment and planning. In particular, we will consider the nature of theatre historiography and what it can reveal about the field of cultural production in Australia.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 1: Introduction to Theatre Historiography This seminar will introduce the course, discuss course scheduling, assessment and planning. In particular, we will consider the nature of theatre historiography and what it can reveal about the field of cultural production in Australia.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 2: The Medea Myth (1)

In this seminar, we consider various ways in which Euripides adapted the existing myths of Medea to create the play we recognize today.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 2: The Medea Myth (1) In this seminar, we consider various ways in which Euripides adapted the existing myths of Medea to create the play we recognize today.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 4: The Medea Myth (2)

In this seminar, we look more comprehensively at ideas of translation and adaptation in theatre, through the lens of Medea.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 4: The Medea Myth (2) In this seminar, we look more comprehensively at ideas of translation and adaptation in theatre, through the lens of Medea.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 5: Immersion and Interactivity (1)

In this seminar, we consider Punchdrunk's ’The Drowned Man’, an adaptation of Georg Büchner's ‘Woyzeck’, to consider how we document and experience immersive and interactive forms of performance.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 5: Immersion and Interactivity (1) In this seminar, we consider Punchdrunk's ’The Drowned Man’, an adaptation of Georg Büchner's ‘Woyzeck’, to consider how we document and experience immersive and interactive forms of performance.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 6: Immersion and Interactivity (2)

In this seminar, we look at theoretical debates regarding participatory performance. Does it invite a liberated form of spectatorship, or is it actually more stultifying than regular theatre?

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 6: Immersion and Interactivity (2) In this seminar, we look at theoretical debates regarding participatory performance. Does it invite a liberated form of spectatorship, or is it actually more stultifying than regular theatre?

learning_activities.topic:
Week 7: Causing Controversy (1)

In this seminar, we will introduce an Early Modern play whose political positions are producing controversial contemporary productions. Students will view a number of these productions.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 7: Causing Controversy (1) In this seminar, we will introduce an Early Modern play whose political positions are producing controversial contemporary productions. Students will view a number of these productions.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 8: Causing Controversy (2)

In this week, we turn to more contemporary controversies, including a reported riot and the meeting of religious and fascist iconography.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 8: Causing Controversy (2) In this week, we turn to more contemporary controversies, including a reported riot and the meeting of religious and fascist iconography.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 9: Conference Paper Workshop

Students will present their ideas for the conference paper to the class for shared feedback.

Learning outcomes: L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 9: Conference Paper Workshop Students will present their ideas for the conference paper to the class for shared feedback.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 10: Conference Paper Presentations (1)

You will present 10 minute 'conference papers' outlining your essay projects.

Learning outcomes: L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 10: Conference Paper Presentations (1) You will present 10 minute 'conference papers' outlining your essay projects.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 11: Conference Paper Presentations (2)

You will present 10 minute 'conference papers' outlining your essay projects.

Learning outcomes: L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 11: Conference Paper Presentations (2) You will present 10 minute 'conference papers' outlining your essay projects.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 12: Australian Theatre Historiography

In this final substantive seminar, we consider the shape of the contemporary Australian field, by looking at the most-produced Australian playwright of the past few years, and surveying the mainstage subscription seasons for 2027.

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 12: Australian Theatre Historiography In this final substantive seminar, we consider the shape of the contemporary Australian field, by looking at the most-produced Australian playwright of the past few years, and surveying the mainstage subscription seasons for 2027.
